lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler and Joey are playing with the duck and the chick.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, wouldn't be cool if our duck and chick had a little baby? We could call it Chuck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Or... Dick.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I-I need a favor. Umm, I was in the shower, and as I was cleansing myself, I ah, I-I, well I felt something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Was it like a sneeze only better?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, I mean, I mean a thing on my body.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (with a disgusted look) What was it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I don't know, it's-it's kinda in a place that's not... It's not visually accessible to me, and I was hoping maybe you guys could-could help me out. (starts to take off his pants)

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Whoa!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on you guys, it's no big deal! (He turns around and shows him his thing.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa-heeeiiiiii-iiiii-ah!! (sees it) Huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well what is it? Is it a mole? (He moves closer to them, and they jump back.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, it's too wrinkly to be a mole.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, eww. What? Is it a pimple?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it's... fancier than a pimple. Look Ross, why don't you just go see a...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering, interrupting them) Hey guys! What's... (sees what they're doing and stops, the guys are stunned)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, well, it's definite, two more weeks of winter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahhh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, right.
Rachel backs out with a confused look on her face.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Chandler, Joey, and Rachel are there, as Phoebe enters with her date.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to her date) Okay, and then this is the coffee house. This is where I play my music. (points to the stage)

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: Good deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, and these are my friends. People. This is Vince, Vince the people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Vince is a fireman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Have you ever rescued anyone from a burning building before?

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: 98 hot saves, highest in the force.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, y'know if Joey and I played with matches we could get you up to an even hundred.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: (dead serious) Fire safety is not a joke, son.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're right, I know.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Look, I gotta go. I'm on call tonight. (kisses her) See you Saturday. (leaves)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (watches him leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, he's cute, Pheebs! But I thought you just started dating that Kindergarten teacher.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Jason? Yeah, uh-huh, we're seeing each other tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What-Pheebs?! Two dates in one day? That's so unlike you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I know! I'm like playing the field. Y'know? Like, juggling two guys, I'm sowing my wild oats. Y'know? Y'know, this kind've like y'know oat-sowin', field-playin' juggler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So Pheebs, do they know about each other?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Does a dog's lips move when he reads? (Joey makes an `I don't know' face, and looks to Chandler and Rachel, who're also stumped) Okay, no they don't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!
He goes over and sits down at the counter, all depressed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (going over to him) Well?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (joining them) Okay, how'd it go at the doctor's?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, he said there's definitely nothing to worry about, it's totally benign.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well what is it?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He couldn't even tell me! He said it was just some sort of skin... abnormality. And the worst thing is he-he-he said, he said, without being able to identify it, he was reluctant to remove it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what? You should go to my guy, because when I went in there with my third nipple. He just lopped it right off. Y'know? So I guess I'm lucky. I mean not as lucky as people who were born with two nipples.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: At least they knew what yours was. Y'know, yours had a name.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Maybe they'll name yours after you! Y'know, they'll call it, The Ross. And then people would be like, "Awww, he's got a Ross."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) Yeah, that'd be cool!
[cut to Phoebe and Rachel as Monica returns from the bathroom]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pete's breaking up with me.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just checked my messages, and he said that when he gets back from Atlanta, we need to talk.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well that's it. People never say `We need to talk' unless it's something bad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, that doesn't necessarily mean that he's breaking up with you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, maybe he just cheated on you.
A Street, Phoebe is walking with her second date, Jason.

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: ...and I know I'll never miss doing it, but I gotta tell you, it's pretty cool knowing that you're making a difference in a kid's life.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so great! Oh, I... (sees that a parked car near them has caught on fire) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: Whoa!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (the fire has worsened) Oh my God!!!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: Ahh-ahh, we'd better call the fire department!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (stopping him) No! No!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: No, no?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, we don't n-n-n-n-need a fireman, we'd, we'd like a good mechanic. (hears the sound of approaching sirens) Oh my God, here they come! Well, we gotta get out of here!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: W-w-w-wait! Why?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well look, if I wanted to see a fireman, I would date one. Okay? (she drags him away)
A Doctor's Office, Ross is having his thing looked at by Dr. Rhodes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Th-th-that's all it is, a third nipple. Y'know? Just your run-of-the-mill third nipple. Y'know? You can take it off. Just slice that baby right off!

lines.speaker: Dr. Rhodes
lines.text: Take your shirt off, and let's see what we're dealing with here. (Ross starts to take off his pants) What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just showing you my run-of-the-mill-slice-it-right-off third nipple.

lines.speaker: Dr. Rhodes
lines.text: Well that's not a third nipple.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Dr. Rhodes
lines.text: First of all, it's on your ass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well then, what is it?!

lines.speaker: Dr. Rhodes
lines.text: Wait a minute, hold it. (He goes to the door and opens it.) Johnson! Will you come in here a moment?

lines.speaker: Dr. Johnson
lines.text: I'm with Hamilton!

lines.speaker: Dr. Rhodes
lines.text: He's good with rear things, bring him in too.
Central Perk, Chandler, Monica, and Joey are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (starting to get up) I gotta go water Pete's plants. (stops) Y'know what, if he's gonna break up with me, maybe I won't water his plants.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, if he's gonna break up with you, maybe Joey and I should water his plants. If y'know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or ha-ha, we could go over there and pee on them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering with Rachel) ...and I-I can't take it! Y'know? I'm just, always afraid one of them is gonna catch me with the other one. It's making me crazy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well honey, then why don't you break up with one of them?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (disgusted) Uh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa. What ah, what happened to playing the field?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it just, it doesn't feel like playing anymore, it feels like work. It's like I'm working in the field.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So Pheebs, pick one of them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. Which one do you like more?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, Vince is great, y'know `cause, he's like a guy, guy. Y'know? He's so burly, he's sooo very burly. (giggles)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, good, so there you go. Go with Vince.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but Jason's really sensitive.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well sensitive is important, pick him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh sure, go with the sissy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Jason is not a sissy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no, I meant Chandler.
Dr. Rhodes's Office, a rather large group of doctors has now gathered to take a look at Ross's thing. Ross is none too pleased with the developments, he has a disgusted look on his face.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know I have dinner plans!!

lines.speaker: Dr. Rhodes
lines.text: Thank you soo much for coming on such a short notice. Ladies and gentlemen, I've-I've-I've been practicing medicine for twenty-three years, and I'm stumped.
He removes the blanket covering the thing.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Whoa. (they all lean in to get a closer look, Ross isn't pleased)
Pete's apartment, Monica is there to water the plants, and is showing the gang around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, this is the den. All right, check this out. Lights! (the lights turn on automatically, but are very bright) Whoa! All right. Less lights! Bad lights! Lights go away! (they dim) Oh, see you just need to find the right command.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, and the dimmer switch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa! For a rich guy he's got, that's a pretty small TV.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no-no, that's a video-phone. But hey guys you're not supposed to be here, so please, do not touch anything.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sitting down on the couch) I-kea! This is comfortable.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering with Phoebe) This place is amazing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God, that is the nicest kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! But it's the nicest kitchen, the refrigerator told me to have a great day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look at this! A millionaire's checkbook.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, put that down! (the phone rings) Oh my God! It's Pete. Okay, get out!! How the hell do you answer a video-phone! (steps in front of it, and automatically answers it)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Monica? (the gang ducks and hides)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess that's how.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Hey Monica, how's it going.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh it's umm, good! It's umm, it's good, just here watering the plants.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well don't forget that fiches over there by Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (standing up) Ahh... Chandler's on the couch!!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I see him, you guys are just the worst hiders ever.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: (standing up) Hey Pete.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi, how ya doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ahh, Pete, the other day when you said you needed to talk, umm, just so I know, is it good news or bad news.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh, it's good news. No, it's definitely good news. Hold on a second, I have another call. (clicks his remote) (to his other call) Hey, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, it's still me.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Ah, no it's not. I've got picture-in-picture here. (to other caller) Yeah. (listens) Yeah, okay. I'm gonna have to call you back later. (pause) Monica? You. I'm gonna have to call you back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, oh, okay umm, so I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Okay, I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: I love you, love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Well, it's good news. It's good news.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what do you thing the good news is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at the checkbook) Wow! Look at this! He wrote a check for 50,000 dollars to Hugo Ligrens Ring Design. (Monica is stunned) Oh, sorry, what do you think the good news is?
[pause]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica's gonna marry a millionaire!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you gotta get Mom on the phone. Call Mom! Call Mom!
Pete's computer automatically calls Mom, Pete's Mom.

lines.speaker: Pete's Mom
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And that's Pete's Mom.
The gang quickly hides again.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, the gang is returning from Pete's.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon you definitely have to make it a theme wedding, and the theme could be, "Look how much money we've got!" Y'know, I mean you could put, you could put money in-in the invitations! You-you could have like little money place settings. And ah, you could start with a money salad! I mean it'll be dry, but people will like it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would you stop? We've only been going out a couple of weeks, I mean we don't even know if he's gonna propose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but this is Pete. Okay? He's not like other people, on your first date he took you to Rome. For most guys that's like a third or fourth date kinda thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well if-if that's what it is, then it's-it's crazy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica's right. We're talking about getting married here. Okay? She-she can't just rush into this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, what do you know! You married a lesbian!
Joey laughs, Ross glares at him, and Joey stops.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. I gotta go. I have break up with Vince.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, so you're going with the teacher, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I like Vince a lot, y'know? But, it's just Jason's so sensitive, y'know? And in the long run, I think sensitive it's just better than having just like a really, really, really nice (pause) butt. (Her eyes glaze over thinking about the butt.) (pause) Jason! Definitely Jason! Okay, wish me luck!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Good luck!
pause

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OH MY GOD!!! (She holds her hands up in triumph and the gang all look at her.) Sorry, I was just imagining what it'd be like to catch the money bouquet.
A Fire House, Phoebe has gone to break up with Vince.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Excuse me. Umm, is Vince here?

lines.speaker: Fireman
lines.text: Oh sure. Vince?!

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: Yo!! (slides down that pole that fire station's have)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! I didn't know you guys actually used those.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: So, what's up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, wow. This-this isn't gonna be easy. Umm, I don't think we should see each other anymore.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: Uh-huh. G-good deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: No-no it's okay. It's just that ah, I thought we had something pretty special here. And y'know I-I felt like you were someone I could finally open up to, and... (starts choking up) That there's so much in me I have to share with you yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, I didn't...

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: (starting to cry) I'm sorry, I can't talk. I'm gonna go write in my journal. (walks away)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (running after him) Wait-wait-wait! Wait!!
Central Perk, Phoebe, Ross, and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Ross) I'm telling you, if you want to take care of that thing, you should go to my herbal guy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, but I want to remove it Pheebs. I don't want to make it savory.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know when girls sleep with guys with weird things on their body, they tell their friends about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gimme this. (Grabs the herbalist's card and leaves.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi! Okay, don't be mad at me, but I couldn't resist.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Brides magazines?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, and I know that you'd say no if he asked you, but I'm sorry; how great would you look walking down the aisle in this Donna Carin. (shows her the picture.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) Oh, you so would! Oh, you should get that anyway. (They both look at her.) Like for clubbing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It is so weird, I know what I said, but uh, this morning, I was lying in bed I was, I was imagining what it would be like to say yes. (Rachel slams the magazine shut in amazement.) I know it's a little sudden, and it's a little rushed, and it's totally not like me to do something like this, but that doesn't mean I can't. Right? I mean I'm-I'm crazy about Pete, and I know that we want the same things, and when I thought about saying yes, it made me really happy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. (starting to cry)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. (pause) I need more pie. (goes and gets some)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Mon umm, if you do get married, can I bring two guests?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You didn't break up with that fireman?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, that was my way of telling you. Well, it turns out he's incredibly sensitive, he keeps a journal and he paints. He even showed me charcoal drawings that he drew of me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well he'd prefer water colors, but y'know, he has easy access to a lot of charcoal.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So then, are you going to dump Jason?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, yeah, because I have to break up with someone, and... Okay so Jason is sensitive, (holds up one finger) but now so's Vince (holds up one finger on her other hand) Plus, Vince has the body y'know? (holds up two more fingers on the Vince side) So... It's really just about the math.
Jason's apartment, Phoebe has gone to break up with Jason.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (knocks on the door) Jason?

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: Yeah, come on in.
She goes in, and sees Jason without his shirt. It turns out that he has a great body too, and is at a loss for words.

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: So Phoebe, you ah, sounded kinda serious on the phone, is ah, is anything wrong?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nah-ha!

Phoebe's Herbal Guy's office, Ross is there about his thing. Ross is looking around the exam room, and he goes over to a large bank of drawers, pulls one out and almost spills it as the herbalist, Guru Saj, enters.

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: You must be Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: I am Guru Saj. (takes the drawer back and replaces)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I got to tell you I've-I've never been to a guru before, so...

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: Well, relax. If it makes you feel better, I've attended some of the finest medical schools in Central America. Well then, let's take a look at this skin abnormality of yours. (motions to the table) Come on, have a seat. (looks at it) Eeh, huh. As I suspected, it's a koondis!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's a koondis?

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: I don't know, what's a koondis with you? (starts laughing as if that joke was funny, Ross only looks at him, and he stops) Please, lie down! I've got a sav that oughta shrink that right up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I guess it's worth a try.

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: Oh sure, we should see results-Whoa!! Clearly not the way to go!! (quickly wipes it off)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! What?!

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: We appear to have angered it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We?! We angered it?!

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: Oh, I think I see the problem. And I'm afraid we're gonna have to use a much stronger tool. (Ross gives him a 'What?' look) Love.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: (He starts moving his hands around in circles above the thing.) Ross, there is absolutely no way this is going to come off unless you start to...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ow!!

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: Oops.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What was, what was that?

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: Well it's gone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! How's that?

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: It got caught on my watch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! (congratulates him)
Pete's apartment, Pete and Monica are coming back from a date.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Lights. (The lights turn on, once again they're too bright.) Uh, romantic lights. (The lights dim.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh, nice.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: So ah, there was this thing I wanted to talk to you about.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, right! I completely forgot about that.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well ah, I've been doing a lot of thinking, and I look at my life...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: And I feel like I've conquered the business world, and I feel like I've conquered the intellectual world, and now I-I have the most beautiful woman in the world.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: There's one thing missing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: It's time for me to conquer the physical world.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (not sure of herself)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Monica, I want to become (pause) the Ultimate Fighting Champion.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You wanna what?!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I want to become the Ultimate Fighting Champion! It's the most intense physical competition in the world, it's banned in 49 states!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Okay, my trainer, Ho Chi, is teaching me a combination of Gee Koon Doe and Brazilian street fighting, I've even had my own octagon training ring designed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And I suppose you used a ring designer for that.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah. Monica, I want you there in the front row when I win. I want you close enough to smell the blood. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My parents will be so happy.
Central Perk, Phoebe's singing, Vince is also there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) "Crazy underwear, creepin' up my butt. (Jason enters) Crazy underwear, always in a rut. Crazy under-(sees Jason)-wear..." (In her head) Oh No! What is he doing here? All right, just keep playing, just keep playing. You'll get through this; you'll be fine. (She tries to continue the song, but she has lost the ability to pronounce words, and the lyrics come out as gibberish.) (giving up on the song) Okay, thank you. And, as always no one talk to me after the show.
They all applaud her.

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: (going up to her) Hey. I was...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: I was passin' by and I saw that you were playing tonight, it's kinda cool seeing you up there. (kisses her)

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: (running over) Whoa! Hey-hey! What's going on here? Who is this guy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know, he just started kissing me. Get him! Get him, Vince!

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, okay, I've-I've been dating both of you, and it's been really horrible. 'Cause y'know it's been a lot of fun, for me. Umm, but I-I like you both, and I, and I didn't know how to chose, so... I'm sorry, I'm just, I'm terrible, I'm a terrible person. I'm terrible.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: Phoebe, Phoebe relax, it's okay. I mean we never said this was exclusive.

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: Yeah, and neither did we. Give yourself a break.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: Yeah. I mean y'know, we haven't been going out that long. Come on, we haven't even slept together yet. Huh.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: You haven't?

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: You have?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, this is none of my business. (starts to walk away)

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: (to Phoebe) I-I can't believe this! You-you've slept with him?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I made you a candle light dinner in the park.

lines.speaker: Jason
lines.text: Y'know Phoebe, I'm gonna make this real easy for you. (walks out)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Vince) Well, that could've been really awkward.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: You made him a candle light dinner in the park?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but I-I-I-I can do that for you, I'm gonna do that for you.

lines.speaker: Vince
lines.text: Uh yeah, I can't believe I ever went out with somebody who would actually have an open flame in the middle of a wooden area. (walks out)
Closing Credits
Guru Saj's office: Joey and Chandler have taken the duck to see the guru.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (comforting the duck) Everything's gonna be all right. Okay, Dick?

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: (entering) Hello, I am Guru Saj-(sees the duck)-Whoa!! (to Joey) That's supposed to be a duck right? 'Cause otherwise, this is waaay out of my league.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. He's got a, he's got a really bad cough, and our vet, he can't do anything about it. Is there something you can do?

lines.speaker: Guru Saj
lines.text: Hmm, let me see. Let me see. Do you think you could get him to eat a bat?
The duck starts to frantically flap his wings, while Joey is holding him, in an attempt to get away.
End
